title: The Component Run
tagline: Four components. Four locations. RUMBLE is already at all of them.
villain_mandate: RUMBLE IS EVERYWHERE THIS EPISODE. He deployed proxy units — not himself — at three locations. He is personally at the fourth. The team cannot win all four runs cleanly. At least one component retrieval must fail or cost the team heavily. RUMBLE's intelligence about where the team would go proves his prediction capability. He wins the intelligence war even if the team gets most components.
structure_note: Split-screen race episode. Four teams, four simultaneous runs. BLAZE leads, calls the split. NOVA watches all four via orbital relay. RUMBLE coordinates proxy units from his construction site. The race is won on precision, not power.

hero_1.name: BLAZE
hero_1.role: Coordinates the split. Runs Location 1 himself. Manages the whole operation via comms. Hardest call: when to pull a team out vs push through.
hero_1.nova_device: NOVA's new resonance counter-device (partial — needs all 4 components to complete)

hero_2.name: STORM
hero_2.role: Location 2 — the speed run. Her location requires extracting a component from a moving target. If anyone can do it, she can.

hero_3.name: GRANITE
hero_3.role: BACK. First mission post-recovery. Location 3 — the hardest physical site, which is why NOVA assigned him there. He is 85% — not 100%. He knows. He goes anyway.
hero_3.visual: Massive grey mech, reinforcement sleeve fully charged — glowing steady amber (healthy), not flickering

support.name: NOVA
support.role: Orbital relay — watches all four runs simultaneously, calls warnings, routes around obstacles. Her 4-second warning times save the team twice.
support.visual: Orbital form, all four feeds visible on her displays

villain_proxies.name: RUMBLE's Proxy Units
villain_proxies.description: Three medium-class autonomous units RUMBLE deployed in advance. Not as powerful as RUMBLE but designed specifically to delay — each unit knows which component the team is targeting and has one job: keep them from getting it until time runs out. They are disposable. RUMBLE built them in 6 hours.
villain_proxies.proxy_1: ANCHOR-class — at Location 1, pins mechs to surfaces using localized gravity (miniature Gravity Anchor tech). Designed for BLAZE.
villain_proxies.proxy_2: INTERCEPT-class — at Location 2, faster than STORM at short distances (not longer). Designed to cut her off.
villain_proxies.proxy_3: CRUSHER-class — at Location 3, brute force. Designed to break reinforced structures GRANITE would use for cover.

location_4.name: RUMBLE himself
location_4.role: At Location 4 personally — the rarest component, the one NOVA couldn't find a substitute for. He knows it's irreplaceable. He's there because he wants NOVA to have to make the call herself.
location_4.note: He doesn't attack when the team arrives. He waits. He's made the location a trap, not a fight.

scenes.title: The Split
scenes.narration: NOVA needed four components. She had located them at four sites across Metron City. She had also detected proxy unit signatures at three of those sites — RUMBLE had deployed in advance. BLAZE looked at the map. Four locations. Three confirmed hostile. One unknown. He assigned STORM to Location 2, GRANITE to Location 3, himself to Location 1. Location 4 was the one with no proxy signature. He said: 'That's the one I'm most worried about.' They split at 06:00.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. TACTICAL BRIEFING — NOVA's holographic map of Metron City with four locations marked. Three show red proxy signatures. One shows nothing — clean. BLAZE looks at that empty one the longest. He assigns each location, pointing. STORM already leaning toward her exit. GRANITE's reinforcement sleeve glowing steady amber — healthy, charged, ready. The clock is visible. 06:00:00. They split. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, tactical briefing scene with holographic city map showing four marked locations — three with red proxy signatures one ominously clean, red-orange mech assigning positions while looking longest at the empty one, silver mech already leaning toward exit, massive grey mech with steady amber-glowing reinforcement sleeve healthy and ready, clock showing 06:00:00, team splitting, 16:9

scenes.title: BLAZE vs ANCHOR
scenes.narration: BLAZE reached Location 1 in ninety seconds. The ANCHOR proxy was already there — medium-class, no weapons, built for one purpose: pinning. It fired a localized gravity pulse. BLAZE's left arm hit the wall. Not fully — he was already moving, already changing angle. NOVA called the counter in 4 seconds: 'Upper right, six meters — the pulse doesn't reach there.' He got clear. Got the component. The proxy fired again. He was already gone.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. BLAZE vs the ANCHOR proxy — a medium boxy unit with gravity emitters. It fires — BLAZE's left arm SLAMS to the wall but he's already pivoting. NOVA's voice call is shown as a display indicator. BLAZE moves to the upper-right gap, snatches the component (a glowing module from a power coupling), the proxy fires again at his previous position. He's four meters clear. Fire-jets igniting. Component secured. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, red-orange mech vs boxy gravity-emitter proxy unit, gravity pulse slamming mech's left arm to wall while already pivoting away, NOVA warning display indicator showing upper right gap, mech moving to gap and snatching glowing component from power coupling, proxy firing at empty previous position, fire-jets igniting with component secured, 16:9

scenes.title: STORM's Moving Target
scenes.narration: Location 2's component was on a transit rail — a moving power conduit traveling between substations at 200 kilometers per hour. The INTERCEPT proxy was designed to be faster than STORM at short distances. It wasn't designed for 3.2 kilometers. STORM matched speed, ran alongside the conduit for eleven seconds, extracted the component at full sprint, and was gone before the proxy could close. It was not graceful. It was exactly fast enough.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. STORM running alongside a high-speed transit rail conduit, neck-and-neck with a sleek INTERCEPT proxy that's slightly faster at close range — but she's maintaining distance. Speed lines everywhere. She extracts the component mid-run — arm reaching, perfect timing — and veers away before the proxy can cut her off. The proxy overshoots. She's already gone. Not a fight. A timing problem solved perfectly. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, silver-blue mech running alongside high-speed transit rail conduit at full sprint with speed lines everywhere, sleek intercept proxy unit slightly ahead at close range, mech reaching out and extracting glowing component from conduit mid-run with perfect timing, veering away before proxy can cut her off, proxy overshooting, she's already gone, not a fight but a timing solution, 16:9

scenes.title: GRANITE Returns
scenes.narration: The CRUSHER proxy at Location 3 was designed to break the reinforced structures GRANITE would use for cover. It knew his fighting style. GRANITE walked into Location 3, saw the proxy, and stood still for two seconds. Then he said: 'I don't need cover.' He charged at 85% power — not full, not safe, exactly enough. The proxy was designed for GRANITE using defensive positioning. It was not designed for GRANITE in the open. He got the component. He was winded. He said he was fine.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. GRANITE CHARACTER MOMENT — he enters Location 3, sees the massive CRUSHER proxy (built to demolish his cover), looks at it for two beats. Then his stance shifts — forward, not defensive. No cover to seek. He charges directly at 85%, reinforcement sleeve blazing amber. The proxy, designed for a GRANITE that hides, is caught off-guard by a GRANITE that doesn't. He gets the component. Then we see him braced against a wall, catching his breath. 85% is enough. Barely. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, massive grey mech entering location seeing massive crusher proxy built to demolish his cover, standing still for two beats then shifting to forward aggressive stance, charging directly with reinforcement sleeve blazing amber, proxy designed for defensive GRANITE caught off-guard by aggressive GRANITE, then mech braced against wall catching his breath after, 85% was enough, 16:9

scenes.title: RUMBLE Is Waiting
scenes.narration: BLAZE reached Location 4 — a decommissioned power relay station — with all three other components secured. Three for four. He needed this one. There was no proxy unit. Just RUMBLE, standing at the center of the station, arms crossed, completely still. The component was visible on a platform behind him. RUMBLE said nothing. BLAZE said: 'You could just let me take it.' RUMBLE said: 'Yes. I could.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. STANDOFF — BLAZE enters the decommissioned power relay station. RUMBLE stands at the center, arms crossed, scarlet visor, completely still. The component — a distinctive power module — sits on a platform directly behind RUMBLE. He hasn't moved it. He hasn't hidden it. He's just standing in front of it. The space between them is twenty meters. BLAZE stops. RUMBLE doesn't move. Two words from RUMBLE, and the scene shifts entirely. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, standoff in decommissioned power relay station — red-orange mech entering to find massive dark-metal villain standing arms crossed with scarlet visor at center, distinctive power module component sitting visibly on platform directly behind him unmoved and unhidden, twenty meters between them, villain completely still and waiting, 16:9

scenes.title: What the Station Does
scenes.narration: NOVA's warning came three seconds after BLAZE entered: the station was live. RUMBLE had reconnected it to the city grid — the same grid he'd mapped in EP005. Every surface was now a conductive trap. BLAZE's fire-jets would cause a feedback pulse through the whole building. He couldn't fight here. He couldn't fly here. He could walk to the component — RUMBLE would let him — and trigger the feedback himself. Or he could leave without it.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. NOVA'S WARNING — BLAZE's display lights up with the station's power map. The entire floor grid is conducting — subtle blue lines of power running through every surface. BLAZE looks at his fire-jets. Looks at RUMBLE. RUMBLE hasn't moved. The component is right there. Twenty meters away. NOVA's analysis is visible: walking to it triggers nothing. Fire or flight triggers a feedback pulse. RUMBLE didn't bring weapons. He brought the environment. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, NOVA warning display showing power map of entire station floor with subtle blue conducting lines everywhere, red-orange mech looking at his fire-jets then at unmoved villain, analysis visible showing walking is safe but fire or flight triggers feedback pulse, villain didn't bring weapons he brought the environment, RUMBLE still perfectly still, 16:9

scenes.title: BLAZE Walks
scenes.narration: BLAZE powered down his fire-jets. He walked. Directly toward RUMBLE. Twenty meters, on foot, no flight, no weapons hot. RUMBLE watched him come. BLAZE reached the platform. He picked up the component. RUMBLE still hadn't moved. They were two meters apart. BLAZE said: 'Why didn't you just move it?' RUMBLE said: 'Because now I know you'll walk into a room I own if the component matters enough.' He stepped aside. BLAZE walked out.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. TENSE WALK — BLAZE, fire-jets fully powered down, walking across the conducting floor directly toward RUMBLE. Twenty meters. Fifteen. Ten. RUMBLE watches, doesn't move. Five meters. BLAZE reaches around RUMBLE — not through him — to the platform. Picks up the component. They're two meters apart. The exchange. Then RUMBLE steps aside. BLAZE walks out. RUMBLE watches him go. His visor: he learned exactly what he came to learn. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, red-orange mech with fire-jets fully powered down walking steadily across conducting floor toward unmoved massive dark villain, counting down twenty meters fifteen ten five, reaching past villain to pick up component from platform while two meters apart, villain watching with visor glow indicating he learned exactly what he came to learn, then stepping aside, mech walking out, 16:9

scenes.title: Four for Four
scenes.narration: All four components. Four locations. Nobody lost. GRANITE was winded. STORM had a proximity scrape from the intercept proxy. BLAZE had walked twenty meters without his fire-jets and his hands wouldn't stop shaking from not using them. NOVA began assembly immediately. Estimated completion: fourteen hours. The Resonance Tower timeline: now under thirty hours. The margin had shrunk. But they had everything they needed.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. TEAM REUNION — all four together with all four components. GRANITE visibly catching his breath but standing strong. STORM with a minor scrape on her chassis. BLAZE's hands are slightly unsteady — the walk cost something even if nothing fired. NOVA already working on assembly, components arranged in front of her. The timer in the background. Thirty hours. Fourteen hours to build. The margin is real but thin. They have what they need. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, team of four reunited with all components — massive grey mech visibly catching breath but standing strong, silver mech with minor chassis scrape, red-orange mech with hands slightly unsteady from what that walk cost, support mech already assembling components arranged before her, countdown timer visible showing thirty hours, fourteen hours to build, thin margin but complete, 16:9

scenes.title: RUMBLE Adjusts the Timeline Again
scenes.narration: RUMBLE returned to the construction site. He updated his timeline. His construction had been at sixty percent when BLAZE arrived at Location 4. It was now at sixty-five percent. He had continued building while BLAZE was walking across the floor. He had not paused construction to be at Location 4. He had been in two places at once — proxy units running the other three sites, his real presence at the station, and his build team operating the entire time. He crossed another line off his list.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. RUMBLE REVEAL — RUMBLE back at the underground construction site. The Resonance Tower framework is visible — more complete than before. He updates his timeline display: 65% complete. A secondary screen shows his build team, who never stopped. He crosses a line off his list. The list has fewer lines than before. The tower framework glows in the background. He was never just guarding the component. He was always building. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, massive dark villain back at underground construction site with Resonance Tower framework more complete than before glowing in background, updating timeline display showing 65% complete, secondary screen showing build team that never stopped, crossing another line off list with fewer remaining, was never just at the station was always building, 16:9

scenes.title: What RUMBLE Knows Now
scenes.narration: NOVA's device assembly would take fourteen hours. The tower had fewer than twenty-eight hours left. A fourteen-hour window. Exactly. NOVA looked at the math for a long moment. She said: 'That's not a coincidence.' BLAZE said: 'He calculated the component run.' NOVA said: 'He calculated me. He knew how long assembly would take. He built his timeline so we'd have exactly one window — and he knows when it opens.' STORM said: 'Then he'll be ready for us.' NOVA said: 'Yes.' Episode 8.
scenes.visual_prompt: Mech Legends dramatic kids action style. FINAL CLIFFHANGER — NOVA's display shows two timelines side by side: her assembly (14 hours) vs the tower completion (28 hours). The gap: exactly 14 hours. Not a coincidence. Not luck. BLAZE's expression: cold realization. STORM's visor showing the calculation. NOVA's face: this is what she feared most — he's not reacting to them. He designed the margin himself. The team has one window. He built the window. He'll be waiting on the other side. 16:9.
scenes.higgsfield_prompt: Dramatic kids action cartoon style, final cliffhanger showing two timelines side by side on display — assembly at 14 hours and tower completion at 28 hours with exactly 14-hour gap between them not coincidence but design, red-orange mech with cold realization expression, silver mech visor showing calculation, support mech with expression of feared-most confirmed, RUMBLE designed the margin himself and will be waiting on the other side, 16:9

music.notes: Scenes 1: tactical build. Scenes 2-4: three separate action themes running in parallel — fast, precise, overlapping. Scene 5: FULL STOP when RUMBLE appears. Silence, then low Rumble theme. Scene 6: tension, no resolution. Scene 7: the walk is the quietest action in any ML episode — almost no music. Just footsteps. Scene 8: brief relief, quickly undercut by the timer. Scene 9: RUMBLE's theme, satisfied. Scene 10: the cliffhanger sting — bigger than EP006's, this is the pre-finale escalation.

voice_style.pacing: Scenes 2-4 narration is fast — split-screen action runs simultaneously. Scene 5-7: slow down completely. 'Because now I know you'll walk into a room I own if the component matters enough.' — pause before and after. Scene 10: each line is a beat. Let 'Yes.' be the last word.
voice_style.character: A narrator tracking four things at once, who goes quiet in scene 7 because walking without fire-jets is the bravest thing BLAZE has done

rumble_wins_audit: RUMBLE wins the intelligence war completely this episode. He: (1) deployed three proxy units perfectly tailored to each hero's weakness, (2) personally held the irreplaceable component and used the environment as his weapon, (3) learned that BLAZE will deprioritize his own capabilities for mission objectives, (4) designed the entire mission timeline including NOVA's assembly window, (5) continued construction throughout. The team got all four components. RUMBLE got exactly the intelligence and timeline control he came for.
granite_return_note: GRANITE at 85% — his return is not triumphant, it's functional. He goes because the mission needs him. The 85% detail matters because in EP008+ it will matter when he has to go beyond it.
youtube_title: 4 Locations. 4 Traps. Can They Get EVERY Component?! ⚡ | Mech Legends Episode 7
youtube_description:
NOVA needs four parts from four locations across Metron City.

RUMBLE deployed proxy units at all of them.

And at Location 4? He's there himself. Waiting. With the rarest component sitting right in front of him.

In today's episode:
⚡ GRANITE returns to action (at 85% — and it's enough. Barely.)
🏃 STORM's impossible moving-target extraction
🔴 BLAZE vs a trap he can't fight or fly through
😰 What RUMBLE actually learned
🕐 The 14-hour window — and who designed it

📺 Watch ALL Mech Legends: [playlist link]
🔔 SUBSCRIBE — Episode 8 next week!

#MechLegends #ComponentRun #RUMBLE #GRANITE #KidsAction
next_episode_preview: Episode 8 — The 14-Hour Window. NOVA's device is ready. They have one chance. RUMBLE designed the window. He'll be there when it opens.
higgsfield_style_notes: The split-screen structure in scenes 2-4 should feel like parallel cuts — the visual style for each location should be distinct: Location 1 (BLAZE) is industrial orange-red, Location 2 (STORM) is blue-silver with speed blur, Location 3 (GRANITE) is grey and amber. Location 4 (RUMBLE) is the darkest, most atmospheric scene in the episode — the power lines in the floor must be subtle but visible once you see them. The walk in scene 7 should feel like the longest twenty meters ever shown.
